headlines.text: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Director of National Intelligence to care for husband battling rare bone cancer
US prepares new military strikes on Iran as Pakistan brokers last-ditch diplomacy in Tehran
SpaceX launches biggest Starship V3 yet on 12th test flight; booster lost, ship splashes into Indian Ocean
Mandalorian and Grogu opens to $33M Friday, eyes $91M-$96M Memorial Day 4-day — lowest Star Wars debut ever
Kyle Busch dies at 41, first active NASCAR Cup driver to pass since Dale Earnhardt in 2001
Trump admin orders green card seekers to leave US and apply from home country in surprise policy reversal
Southern California chemical tank crisis: 40,000 under evacuation as 34,000-gallon MMA tank risks explosion
China coal mine gas explosion in Shanxi kills at least 90 workers
Dow hits record close at 50,579 as markets cheer Iran peace progress and eighth straight winning week
Gas prices hit 4-year Memorial Day high at $4.56/gal as Strait of Hormuz stays closed

breaking.text: Pakistan army chief holds emergency talks with Iranian leadership in Tehran; Rubio says deal possible 'later today or tomorrow'

stories.hed: Peace Talks on a Knife's Edge as Trump Skips Son's Wedding to Stay in War Room
stories.dek: Pakistan's army chief flew to Tehran for late-night talks with Iran's foreign minister while Rubio, mid-India-trip, told reporters a deal could come 'later today or tomorrow.' Trump, who canceled his Memorial Day weekend plans and will miss Don Jr.'s Bahamas wedding, is reviewing fresh strike options in case diplomacy implodes — because nothing says family values like a wartime situation room.

stories.hed: China Coal Mine Explosion Kills at Least 90 in Shanxi Province
stories.dek: A gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province killed at least 90 people Friday evening with approximately 247 workers on duty at the time. Chinese state media reported rescue operations are ongoing.

stories.hed: Trump Admin Upends Green Card System: Foreigners Must Leave US to Apply
stories.dek: In a surprise Friday announcement, the Trump administration reversed a longstanding policy by requiring foreigners already living in the US who seek green cards to depart and apply from their home countries. Immigration attorneys called it one of the most disruptive policy shifts in decades.

stories.hed: Judge Calls DOJ Prosecution of Kilmar Abrego Garcia 'Vindictive,' Dismisses Charges
stories.dek: A federal judge threw out human smuggling charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia, ruling the DOJ reopened the case after he sued over his deportation — conduct the court labeled 'vindictive prosecution.' The DOJ immediately vowed to appeal, ensuring this one stays in the news cycle indefinitely.

stories.hed: SpaceX IPO Filing Drops the Same Week as Starship's Biggest Test — Musk Doing Musk Things
stories.dek: Two days after Elon Musk filed to take SpaceX public at a $1.75 trillion Nasdaq valuation — potentially the largest IPO in history — he launched the most powerful rocket ever built and called the fiery Indian Ocean splashdown 'epic.' Whether this is visionary timing or the most expensive product launch event ever staged is left as an exercise for investors.

markets.context: Stocks closed at fresh records Friday as Iran peace-talk optimism offset Treasury yield concerns, giving Wall Street its eighth straight winning week.

gas.note: Filling up for Memorial Day weekend is costing you $1.38 more than last year — the Strait of Hormuz thanks you for your service.

popCulture.hed: Star Wars Returns: Mandalorian and Grogu Opens to $33M Friday, Projected $91M-$96M Memorial Day Weekend
popCulture.dek: The galaxy far, far away is back on the big screen for the first time since 2019, with Pedro Pascal and everyone's favorite green toddler drawing solid but franchise-low crowds. Critics gave it 63% on Rotten Tomatoes while audiences handed it 88% — the usual Star Wars civil war, now in theaters.

genZ.term: Devil Wears Prada Miranda Priestly 'and Emily... that's all' trend
genZ.definition: Creators are using the iconic dismissive line from the Devil Wears Prada 2 — which opened May 1 and is still dominating the cultural conversation — to contrast two people or situations, with one side polished and correct and the other a disaster. It has exploded on TikTok as the universal format for calling out any two-tier situation with maximum passive-aggressive elegance.
genZ.usage: Iran negotiators showing up to talks vs. Iran negotiators walking out: 'Iran's chief negotiator... and Emily, that's all.'

tldr: Day 85 of the Iran war ended with Pakistan flying into Tehran for emergency diplomacy while Trump skipped his son's wedding to stare at strike options — a deal may or may not exist by the time you read this. Meanwhile, Americans hit the road for Memorial Day paying $4.53 a gallon, Baby Yoda opened in theaters to franchise-low numbers, and Tulsi Gabbard quietly handed in her resignation.
